title: Planetary shield generator
content:
{{Top|leg}}
'''Planetary shield generators''' were powerful [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|deflector shield generators]] capable of protecting [[Planet/Legends|planets]] or [[City/Legends|cities]] from [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|orbital bombardment]]. Two main types of [[Planetary shield/Legends|planetary shield]] existed; encasing shields and shutter shields.<ref name="HS">''[[Hideouts & Strongholds]]''</ref> 

Encasing shields were an older model and were either on or off at any one time. They provided a solid wall of protection but did not allow defenders to fire out of the shield. Designated gaps known as firing slots or firing gaps were permanent openings in the shield, and were high priority targets during an attack. Encasing shields were cheaper and more common, and easier to maintain. In addition to blocking weapons fire, encasing shields blocked [[Sensor/Legends|sensors]]. An example of an encasing shield was [[MerrWeapons]]' [[WorldArmor 9 Planetary Shield]].<ref name="HS" />

Shutter shields were interlocking shields that could cover large sections of a planet. They were not solid walls but were adjustable, capable of overlapping and combining to allow the defenders to fire out at the enemy, or allow [[Starship/Legends|starships]] and [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] to leave. [[DefenStar Ltd./Legends|DefenStar Ltd.]] manufactured the [[Nyalsan II Planetary Shield]], an example of a shutter shield.<ref name="HS" />

The [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] used a [[CoMar Combat Systems/Legends|CoMar]] [[SLD-26 planetary shield generator/Legends|SLD-26 planetary shield generator]] on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Endor/Legends|Endor]]. The generator was used to project a defensive shield around the [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]] which was in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]].<ref name="EGWT">''[[The Essential Guide to Weapons and Technology]]''</ref>

==Sources==
*<!-- 1988-10-XX -->''[[Assault on Hoth]]''
